cases.title: Testing-plan check-in eligibility row: not-enrolled student rejected at roster lookup
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Check-in eligibility and duplicate block applies (FR-17, FR-18, BR-06, BR-07, AC-07, AC-08).
Open class session exists with valid QR token; authenticated student fixture has no Active enrollment in the target class section.
steps: Authenticate as a student who is not actively enrolled in the session's class section.
Submit check-in with a valid qrToken for the open session.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Check-in is rejected with outcome NotEnrolled per BR-06; a failed CheckInAttempt is logged and no Present/Late AttendanceRecord is created.

cases.title: Enrollment roster lookup at M03/M04 boundary rejects check-in without attendance write
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for enrollments, check_in_attempts, and attendance_records tables.
Open session with valid QR token exists; student actor is authenticated but enrollment row is absent or status is Dropped for the session section.
steps: Invoke check-in command with valid qrToken and resolved student actor lacking Active enrollment in target classSectionId.
Query enrollments, CheckInAttempt, and AttendanceRecord tables within the same transaction boundary.
Repeat with Active-enrolled student actor as positive control.
cases.expected: Not-enrolled invocation persists one failed CheckInAttempt with outcome NotEnrolled and creates no success AttendanceRecord; Active-enrolled control passes enrollment gate (DI-03) to subsequent validation steps.

cases.title: CheckInAttempt SM-04 terminates at NotEnrolled without attendance mutation
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in integration test database.
Authenticated student has no Active enrollment for the session's class section.
steps: Capture beforeState: no Present/Late AttendanceRecord for the student-session pair.
Submit check-in command that passes authentication, session Open, and token Valid guards.
Capture afterState for CheckInAttempt outcome and AttendanceRecord rows.
cases.expected: SM-04 evaluation reaches NotEnrolled terminal state at enrollment guard (validation order step 4); afterState shows persisted failed attempt with NotEnrolled and no attendance status mutation.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins returns 422 envelope with NotEnrolled for unenrolled student
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated student bearer token belongs to a user without Active enrollment in the session section.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP status, response envelope (data/meta/error), and error.code.
Verify no success attendance payload is returned.
cases.expected: API returns 422 with standard envelope where data is null and error.code is NotEnrolled; response includes classSessionId in details where applicable and no attendance success outcome.

cases.title: Alternate path: admin enrollment correction enables subsequent check-in while session Open
preconditions: Open class session exists with valid QR token.
Student initially has no Active enrollment in the target section.
Academic Admin has scope to import or upsert enrollment for the section.
steps: Student submits POST /v1/check-ins and receives NotEnrolled rejection with failed attempt logged.
Academic Admin calls POST /v1/enrollments/import to add student with Active status for the class section.
Student re-submits POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken while session remains Open.
Query AttendanceRecord and CheckInAttempt for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: First attempt fails per BR-06 with NotEnrolled; after enrollment correction per BR-06 exception path, second attempt passes enrollment gate and may proceed to downstream rules (duplicate, GPS, status assignment) without prior NotEnrolled block.

cases.title: Boundary error for Dropped enrollment, absent row, and cross-section roster mismatch
preconditions: Student A has Active enrollment only in section X.
Open session exists in section Y with valid QR token.
Student B has Dropped enrollment row in section Y; Student C has no enrollment row in section Y.
steps: Student A (enrolled in X only) submits POST /v1/check-ins with section Y session token.
Student B (Dropped in Y) submits POST /v1/check-ins with the same section Y token.
Student C (no enrollment row in Y) submits POST /v1/check-ins with the same section Y token.
Inspect HTTP status and error.code for all requests and persisted attempt records.
cases.expected: All three requests return 422 with error.code NotEnrolled; each creates a failed CheckInAttempt with structured reason and no success AttendanceRecord regardless of Dropped vs absent enrollment row.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: NotEnrolled returned before duplicate and GPS checks
preconditions: Open session exists with valid QR token.
Unenrolled student is authenticated.
Same student has prior successful attendance in a different session OR GPS would fail if enrolled—conditions that would fail BR-07 or BR-08 to BR-10 if enrollment passed.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins as unenrolled student who already has success record in another session.
Assert error.code is NotEnrolled, not DuplicateCheckIn.
Submit as unenrolled student with GPS payload outside radius on GPS-required policy session.
Assert error.code is NotEnrolled, not OutOfRadius or GpsDisabled.
Submit duplicate-prone replay with same Idempotency-Key for unenrolled student.
Assert error.code remains NotEnrolled, not DuplicateCheckIn.
cases.expected: Validation order per API design §5.1 and validation rules §2.2: enrollment check (BR-06 step 4) short-circuits before BR-07 duplicate and BR-08 to BR-10 GPS evaluations when student lacks Active enrollment.

cases.title: Student export denied and cross-scope Lecturer cannot access non-assigned section roster
preconditions: Open session exists in section A assigned to Lecturer-A with enrolled students.
Lecturer-B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
Student account is authenticated; Student has Active enrollment in section B only.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage.
Lecturer-B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for section A session.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden.
Student (enrolled in B only) calls POST /v1/check-ins with section A qrToken.
Assert HTTP 422 NotEnrolled without leaking section A roster data.
cases.expected: Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied roster read per BR-19; unenrolled-section check-in denied with NotEnrolled per BR-06 without partial restricted data in any response.

cases.title: Idempotent replay for not-enrolled student returns consistent NotEnrolled not DuplicateCheckIn
preconditions: Open session exists with enrolled students and valid QR token.
Unenrolled student actor is authenticated with no Active enrollment in target section.
Test harness can replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key, actor, and token.
steps: Submit first POST /v1/check-ins as unenrolled student; record NotEnrolled outcome.
Replay identical request with same Idempotency-Key immediately.
Submit parallel duplicate requests with same key from harness.
Query check_in_attempts and attendance_records for student-session.
Confirm no Present/Late success row exists.
cases.expected: All replays return deterministic NotEnrolled per idempotency rules §7.1; BR-07 DuplicateCheckIn is not evaluated because BR-06 short-circuits first at enrollment guard; no success attendance record created.

cases.title: UI journey surfaces NotEnrolled danger alert with academic-office guidance and no retry
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser (PG-02 check-in page).
Open session QR token is valid; student is not actively enrolled in the target section.
steps: Navigate to check-in URL with valid token query parameter.
Submit check-in from the student check-in form.
Observe CheckInResultScreen failure state and alert component styling.
Verify absence of retry CTA and presence of contact academic office guidance per FLOW-02 and BR-06 outcome.
cases.expected: UI renders failure-not-enrolled danger alert with localized Vietnamese message (e.g. 'Bạn không thuộc lớp học phần này.'); no retry button is shown; student cannot reach success-present or success-late state; message instructs student to contact academic office if roster is incorrect.
